name: Mirador del Embalse de Aguilar — circular desde Aguilar de Campoo
region_intro: Hiking around Aguilar De Campoo offers diverse natural landscapes in the north of Palencia, Spain. The region is characterized by high-altitude limestone moorlands, impressive river canyons, and unique rock formations within the UNESCO Global Geopark Las Loras. The expansive Aguilar de Campoo Reservoir is a central feature, alongside prominent peaks like Peña Cutral and Monte La Ruya, providing varied terrain for hikers. Best hiking trails around Aguilar De Campoo The most popular hiking route is Mirador del Embalse de Aguilar — circular desde Aguilar de Campoo, a moderate 4.6 miles (7.5 km) trail that takes about 2 hours to complete, offering views of the reservoir. Another top favourite among local hikers is Peña Mesa y Castillo de Gama por el Cañón de la Horadada — circular por el Espacio Natural de las Tuerces, a difficult 13.4 miles (21.5 km) path. This route explores the Cañón de la Horadada and the Espacio Natural de las Tuerces, featuring significant elevation changes. Local hikers also love the City tour por Aguilar de Campoo, a 2.0 miles (3.3 km) trail leading through the town center, often completed in about 52 minutes. Hiking around Aguilar De Campoo is defined by limestone moorlands, dramatic river canyons, and the expansive reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy town walks to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Aguilar De Campoo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 120 reviews. More than 290 hikers have used komoot to explore Aguilar De Campoo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there around Aguilar de Campoo?
A: Aguilar de Campoo offers a diverse network of over 30 hiking trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy strolls through the town to challenging ascents in the surrounding natural parks.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for families in Aguilar de Campoo?
A: Yes, Aguilar de Campoo has several easy trails perfect for families. For a gentle introduction to the town's charm, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067803620/city-tour-por-aguilar-de-campoo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;City tour por Aguilar de Campoo&lt;/a&gt;, a 3.3 km route. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1176686779/ermita-de-santa-eulalia-y-barrio-de-san-pedro-desde-barrio-de-santa-maria-circular-por-el-camino-natural-del-romanico-palentino" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Santa Eulalia y Barrio de San Pedro&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.5 km and explores the Camino Natural del Románico Palentino.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Aguilar de Campoo?
A: The region is part of the UNESCO World Geopark Las Loras, offering a striking variety of landscapes. You'll encounter high-altitude limestone moorlands, dramatic river canyons, and unique rock formations. The expansive Aguilar de Campoo Reservoir is a central feature, providing scenic views, especially of Peña Cutral.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Aguilar de Campoo?
A: Yes, many trails in Aguilar de Campoo are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular moderate circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1327919180/mirador-del-embalse-de-aguilar-circular-desde-aguilar-de-campoo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Embalse de Aguilar — circular desde Aguilar de Campoo&lt;/a&gt;, offering beautiful reservoir views over 7.5 km.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks to look out for on the trails?
A: Hikers can discover several unique natural attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4045496" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Horadada Tunnel&lt;/a&gt; is a fascinating geological feature. For a truly ancient experience, visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5596202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tejeda de Tosande Yew Forest Trail&lt;/a&gt;, home to ancient yew trees. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4848389" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covalagua Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is another impressive natural highlight.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: For experienced trekkers seeking a challenge, the region offers more demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067760816/pena-mesa-y-castillo-de-gama-por-el-canon-de-la-horadada-circular-por-el-espacio-natural-de-las-tuerces" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Mesa y Castillo de Gama por el Cañón de la Horadada&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 21.5 km path with significant elevation changes, exploring the Espacio Natural de las Tuerces.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Aguilar de Campoo?
A: The hiking trails around Aguilar de Campoo are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 120 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning views of the reservoir, and the unique geological formations found within the UNESCO Global Geopark Las Loras.

Q: Can I see any historical or cultural sites while hiking?
A: Yes, many hiking paths are intertwined with the region's rich cultural heritage, particularly its Romanesque architecture. The Camino Natural del Románico Palentino is a prime example, allowing hikers to appreciate both natural beauty and numerous medieval churches and monasteries, such as those near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4848732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa María la Real Monastery&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there opportunities for wildlife spotting on the trails?
A: The natural environment around Aguilar de Campoo, particularly areas like Peña Cutral, offers opportunities to observe rich flora and fauna. Keep an eye out for birds such as Egyptian vultures and white storks, which are known to inhabit the area.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Aguilar de Campoo?
A: While the guide doesn't specify a single 'best' time, the diverse landscapes mean hiking can be enjoyed across seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer is ideal for exploring higher altitudes, and during periods of low water, the submerged village of Cenera de Zalima becomes visible in the reservoir, adding a unique historical dimension.

Q: Are there any viewpoints offering panoramic views of the area?
A: Yes, the region boasts several scenic viewpoints. &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3932476" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valcabado Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offers expansive vistas. Additionally, prominent peaks like Peña Cutral and Monte La Ruya provide panoramic views of the reservoir and the surrounding natural environment after a moderate ascent.

Q: Are there any trails that explore the unique rock formations of the region?
A: Absolutely. The Espacio Natural de las Tuerces is particularly known for its unique rock formations. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31875361" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Horadada Tunnel – Peña Mesa loop from Olleros de Pisuerga&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 15 km route that takes you through this fascinating landscape, allowing you to experience these geological wonders firsthand.
tips: The Monastery of Santa María la Real is a Romanesque building built for the most part between the 12th and 13th centuries. During the Middle Ages it became one of the main centers of artistic and cultural irradiation in the area. It was inhabited by Premonstratensian monks until the confiscation of Mendizábal, when it was declared abandoned. For this reason, the following year, 1866, it was declared a National Monument, a fact that did not prevent most of its capitals from being taken to the MAN. It will not be until 1978 when the restoration and rehabilitation works are really effective, leaving a space shared by an Institute, the ROM, Romanesque and Territory exhibition center (a new concept to show Romanesque art) and the Library of the Santa María la Real Foundation.
Great viewpoint tower that allows us to enjoy panoramic views of the Aguilar Reservoir and its dam. On the horizon you can see the Palencia mountains.
Puente Mayor over the Pisuerga River is of medieval origin. We can see the small islands in the river and the castle hill.
